feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: First West Nile Virus-Positive Mosquitoes Reported In Chicago This Year
entry.summary: No humans have tested positive, per the Chicago Department of Public Health. The best way to prevent the illness is to prevent mosquito bites.

entry.title: What would it take to stop women from bleeding to death after childbirth?
entry.summary: A newly published series of reports calls attention to a dire situation facing millions of women after childbirth — and the solutions that can prevent death from postpartum hemorrhage.

entry.title: Fourth Person Infected by Emerging Tick-Bourne Disease
entry.summary: Another California resident tested positive for the bacteria Rickettsia lanei, as confirmed by The California Department of Public Health. This marks the...

entry.title: Californian Dies from Rare Disease After 'Trapping, Feeding and Breeding Wild Rats' in an RV
entry.summary: Another person fell ill in the incident, which the city manager said was an "extreme situation"

entry.title: Scientist creates 'mini‑universe' to measure time without a clock
entry.summary: A University of Birmingham scientist has built a "mini-universe" that takes a step toward answering one of science's biggest questions: "What is time?" Publishing his findings in Physical Review Research, Professor Giovanni Barontini shows how it is possible …

entry.title: Bay Area still battling 'extremely contagious' norovirus
entry.summary: Traces of norovirus are at high levels in Palo Alto, San Jose, Fremont, Vallejo and Novato, June 11 wastewater data shows.

entry.title: A 1998 Boston party photo shows a woman with her boyfriend. Her future husband is in it too.
entry.summary: She posted a film photo from her last college apartment party in 1998. The caption is a whole love story in one sentence.

entry.title: Doctor shares 5 daily habits that can lower elevated liver enzymes and protect from fatty liver disease | Health
entry.summary: Fatty liver disease is getting increasingly common, especially due to poor lifestyle habits. Dr Sood shares 5 easy ways you can protect your liver. | Health

entry.title: The Age You Lost Your Virginity May Define How You Age In Later Life, Study Reveals
entry.summary: Researchers in China found that earlier intimate activity may affect people’s aging process.

entry.title: We Asked Dietitians to Name the Healthiest Packaged Snack—They All Chose the Same One
entry.summary: Of all the choices for the healthiest packaged snack, dietitians say roasted chickpeas rise to the top.

entry.title: Odds & Ends: June 12, 2026
entry.summary: Your dad bod could raise your kids’ risk for obesity and disease. You’ve probably heard of the “dad bod.” It’s the body shape of a guy who’s not super fat, but not in particularly good shape, either. It’s gotten a friendly rebrand over the past decade with ar…

entry.title: 4 Fruits to Help Reduce Heart Disease Risk, According to a Cardiologist
entry.summary: Cardiologists share the four fruits they recommend patients incorporate into their regular diet to support heart health.

entry.title: Stanford scientists regrow lost cartilage and reverse arthritis in major breakthrough
entry.summary: A new treatment that blocks an aging-related protein restored lost cartilage in old mice and helped prevent arthritis after knee injuries. Human cartilage samples showed similar signs of regeneration, raising hopes for a future drug that could repair joints i…

entry.title: Magic mushrooms and Alzheimer’s: what one remarkable case can tell us
entry.summary: A woman with advanced dementia appeared to regain speech and independence after psilocybin. The findings are intriguing, but far from proof.

entry.title: Millennial Hygiene Habits Boomers Just Don't Understand
entry.summary: Some of the millennial hygiene habits that go against boomer tradition include avoiding antiperspirants, embracing body hair, and not buying paper napkins.

entry.title: Turmeric vs. Magnesium: Which Helps More With Reducing Inflammation?
entry.summary: Both turmeric and magnesium can offer health benefits, but which is better for inflammation, heart health, and relaxation?

entry.title: Mind-blowing simulation shows extreme impact that fasting for 36 hours has on your body
entry.summary: It comes as intermittent fasting becomes popular on social media.

entry.title: Scientists Found a New Alzheimer's Trigger, And a Drug to Slow It in Mice
entry.summary: Dementia remains stubbornly resistant to treatment and devastating for millions of people worldwide.

entry.title: 5 Supplements You Shouldn't Take to Prevent Cancer, According to a Dietitian
entry.summary: When it comes to cancer prevention, a dietitian explains why supplements aren't always helpful—and can sometimes be risky.

entry.title: Surprising ways menopause can affect your mouth
entry.summary: Hormone changes during menopause can mess with your dental and oral health. Here’s how experts recommend managing the effects.

entry.title: Ebola cases in DRC rise to 676 as Kenya protests erupt over US plans
entry.summary: Outbreak responses are still playing catch-up as US works to isolate itself.

entry.title: Vitamin C may help preserve brain gray matter volume as we age
entry.summary: Low levels of vitamin C in blood plasma was linked to lower brain gray matter volume and connectivity during aging in a new study.

entry.title: Vitamin A Toxicity Increased After RFK Jr. Suggested It As A Measles Treatment
entry.summary: New research found online searches for the vitamin and "measles" increased last year. Here’s what you need to know.

entry.title: This might tick you off: It’s going to be a big year for your least-favorite bloodsuckers
entry.summary: A mild winter and a warm spring could make for a big tick season in Colorado, and that could mean more cases of tick-borne illnesses.

entry.title: These patients' hearts stopped a dozen times a day. An innovative procedure has transformed their lives.
entry.summary: People with a rare condition experience "pauses" in their heart beat that cause them to faint. A new procedure could change their lives, research suggests.

entry.title: The people of Okinawa, Japan only eat until they are about 80 percent full, then stop — and the practice has been linked in multiple peer-reviewed studies to lower rates of cardiovascular disease, slower biological aging, and a measurable extension of healthy
entry.summary: There is a phrase that Okinawans of an older generation often murmur to themselves before sitting down to eat: hara hachi bu. The Chinese characters mean, more or less, “stomach eight parts,” and the full sense of the saying is “eat until your stomach is eigh…

entry.title: Infectious disease threats test public health
entry.summary: As Trump officials step up efforts against New World screwworm, health experts warn funding cuts may weaken disease responses.

entry.title: Scientists found the strength training sweet spot for a longer life
entry.summary: Just 90–120 minutes of strength training a week may deliver some of the biggest long-term health rewards, according to a study tracking more than 147,000 people for 30 years. That amount was linked to lower risks of death overall, particularly from cardiovasc…

entry.title: A Major Update Just Hit Cholesterol Guidelines – Here’s What Every Adult Needs To Know
entry.summary: New ACC/AHA cholesterol guidelines promote earlier detection, personalized risk assessment, and more aggressive LDL cholesterol lowering to reduce long-term cardiovascular disease risk.

entry.title: Popular joint pain supplement might increase Alzheimer's risk, study says
entry.summary: A popular over-the-counter supplement taken for joint pain might increase people's risk for Alzheimer's disease, a new study says.

entry.title: Men encouraged to stay on top of screenings, preventive care
entry.summary: June is Men's Health Month, which aims to raise awareness about preventive care and common health screenings for men.

entry.title: 'I've never been this good' – revolutionary immune reset puts lupus in remission
entry.summary: Patients on the trial have not needed medication to manage their condition.

entry.title: Meningitis B vaccine to be offered to thousands of young people in July
entry.summary: The decision for the one-off vaccine programme follows the unprecedented outbreak in Kent this year.

entry.title: Berkeley death from rat-linked infection serves as 'wake-up call,' expert says
entry.summary: On Wednesday, Berkeley city officials said a person died in May after contracting leptospirosis months after they issued a warning on the disease.

entry.title: Potential case of rare and deadly hantavirus investigated at Bay Area prison
entry.summary: Hantavirus, a "stealth infection," made recent headlines after an outbreak on a cruise ship.

entry.title: Patients suffering from long-term Lyme disease symptoms face difficulties
entry.summary: Researchers are studying post-treatment Lyme disease syndrome and why some patients have symptoms for months or years after being infected.

entry.title: Collagen vs Hyaluronic Acid: Which Is Best For Anti-Aging?
entry.summary: The efficacy of collagen vs hyaluronic acid has long been debated among skincare experts. Vogue explores which of these hero ingredients is best for anti-aging.

entry.title: As Tick Issues Soar, Fear Becomes a Factor
entry.summary: For stakeholders in the Island’s economy — from food and hospitality to recreation — striking a balance between tick awareness and not spreading too much fear has become something of a highwire act.

entry.title: CDC and Florida at odds over hantavirus cruise ship passenger’s quarantine
entry.summary: Florida health officials are pushing back at quarantine guidance from the CDC for passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius exposed to the Andes hantavirus.

entry.title: New Antibiotic Found in Dirt Targets Superbugs in a Way ‘Never Been Seen Before’
entry.summary: Researchers tested their newly discovered peptide against one common antibiotic-resistant bacteria. Only about one in every hundred million survived to pass on genetic resistance.

entry.title: Deer, dog ticks are abundant in Maine right now. Here’s what to do about them.
entry.summary: Your frequently asked questions about ticks are answered

entry.title: Socioeconomic Status Leaves Deep Imprints on Developing Brains
entry.summary: A new study, analyzing 12,000 children proves socioeconomic factors drive 16% of brain development, completely outpacing IQ.

entry.title: Menopause hormone therapy: Demand rises but training gaps remain
entry.summary: As well as acute shortages in supply, there are other significant gaps in research and the training of health professionals.

entry.title: White House response to hantavirus and Ebola contrasts with COVID criticisms
entry.summary: The administration imposed mandatory quarantine orders on two passengers from the cruise ship hit by hantavirus and is blocking Americans who catch Ebola from returning home for treatment.

entry.title: There’s a Teenage Milestone That I Couldn’t Wait to Achieve. My Son Is Actively Avoiding It.
entry.summary: Every kid couldn't wait for this, I thought.

entry.title: Hypothalamic Histamine Waves Open and Close Memory Access
entry.summary: A new study proves spontaneous hypothalamic histamine fluctuations gate moment-to-moment memory accessibility.

entry.title: Can What You Eat Actually Help Prevent Dementia?
entry.summary: Experts explain whether what’s on your plate can make a meaningful difference in your dementia risk. Plus, healthy recipes to put it into practice.

entry.title: Genomic landscape of the human vaginal microbiome is linked to host genetics and population of origin
entry.summary: Metagenomics, coupled with genome-wide association analyses, highlights population-specific patterns of variation within the vaginal microbiome and identifies host genetic loci associated with vaginal microbiome composition.

entry.title: ‘Huge’ 8-Week-Old Golden Retriever Puppy Has the Entire Vet Office Obsessed
entry.summary: This precious puppy is popular.

entry.title: What’s the Future of Gene Editing?
entry.summary: In the first episode of the new season of ‘The Joy of Why,’ Nobel Laureate Jennifer Doudna discusses how she discovered CRISPR’s genome-editing power, the breakthroughs and hurdles during its explosive growth, and what lies ahead for this groundbreaking techn…
